reviews.text:
After watching a beautiful sunrise, we decided to try this breakfast spot as it looked very promising. I called ahead and was told they open at 7:00 am. When we arrived at exactly 7:00, the manager informed us that they were not open yet. She then asked what time it was, and when we said 7:00, told us we could only sit inside as the courtyard was fully booked.

Three of us ordered the eggs Benedict. My mother specifically requested well-done eggs but received runny poached eggs instead. The homemade rosti served with all the Benedicts was extremely hard, difficult to cut, and had a burnt taste. I struggled to eat mine and eventually gave up.

My daughter ordered the waffles, which according to the menu are served with Nutella, grilled banana, and pecan caramel sauce. The dish arrived with no Nutella at all, and the waffles themselves tasted like a banana bread batter — which is not described or mentioned anywhere on the menu (see menu image).

Overall, this was the worst breakfast experience we’ve had. Very disappointing, especially given how promising the venue initially appeared.

All this while a child puked and they took 15 minutes to clean it up

Lastly the waiters and managers argued over the R200 tip a tourist had just handed to one of them 🤮
reviews.author: Roxanne Manacas

reviews.text:
Breakfast a bit overpriced and small.
Also more than advertised on their website which may be an oversight but not good enough for a luxury establishment like the Winchester Hotel in Sea Point. Overall disappointing as the competition is so high in this area. I think they are focused on international visitors.
reviews.author: Greg Lock

reviews.text: Тирамису был очень плохой, печенье внутри совершенно сухое тесто , крем слишком жидкий, это просто худший вариант что я пробывала. Плюс самый высокий ценник в городе, но качество отсутствует. У этого десерта простой рецепт, его приготовит даже школьник . И к такому качеству еды возвращаться не хочется сюда . Пиши этот отзыв чтобы вы начали вкусно делать десерты. Потому что сегодня это полный провал
reviews.author: Полина Силина

reviews.text: Очень уютный двор, приятная атмосфера
reviews.author: Tatiana Muchkina

reviews.text:
We come here weekly and it’s honestly a hidden gem. Love the chicken kebab, the roasted cabbage, the fries with truffle and the house salad.

Wine is just overpriced.
reviews.author: Roos Ates

reviews.text: Nice restaurant.  Riblets were great.  So was the tuna.  Service was better than most restaurants here.
reviews.author: James N

reviews.text: Super slow service. So many waiters but all of them look disorganised and confused.
reviews.author: Michael Parkes

reviews.text: Great sunsets
reviews.author: Geoff Croxford

reviews.text: We ended up here “by mistake” when our regular munch spot was closed on Christmas Eve. We opted for a few starters and bar snacks. Everything was so delish. The focaccia is this wild fire roasted bread. So lovely. We threw in some smoked nuts with our spicey margarita cocktails - really good combo. The kids had chocolate and vanilla milkshakes and fries. The fries come with a perinaise dip. Super delish. And my fave was the haloumi phyllo pasty pockets… now this is something I could eat every day of the week! I recommend this spot for sunset and bar snacks and bites!!! Amazing
reviews.author: Janet Hofman

reviews.text: Went there for a Sunday lunch to celebrate a special birthday, sat in the courtyard. Had sharing plates and main meals. Good portions. Halal friendly.
reviews.author: Adenaan Hardien

reviews.text: I was at Shoreditch last week Sunday for My with My Husband and friends. Beautiful place. The Food was delicious 🤤 service was great 😊
reviews.author: Felicia Erasmus

reviews.text: I don't usually hand out 5-star reviews, but wow – I've gotta give credit where it's due on this one. Honestly, this was such an amazing experience. Even living here, you sometimes forget what makes this place special until something reminds you all over again. This totally did that for me. The pavlova was the best I have tasted.  Will definitely be back for more.
reviews.author: Tracy-Lee Anderson

reviews.text:
I first came here for brunch in their stunning courtyard. I went back to try their lunch.

The staff were very friendly and patient, and the service was great. Their food for their lunch menu is more extensive compared to their breakfast menu.

I will say that their food is very average for their prices but the atmosphere is unbeatable.

Will recommend stopping by for a drink and quick bite if youe find yourself in this part of the city.
reviews.author: Wandile Ngwenya

reviews.text:
We had lunch at Shoreditch @The Winchester.
The food was slightly above average and expensive. Nicely plated food though.
The service was a bit slow.

I think it’s a good place for a drink with a friend as there is an outdoor area with a view. It’s relaxing too. I wouldn’t necessarily rush there again though to eat the food.
reviews.author: Chante Cheriese Crawford

reviews.text: Very nice staff, recommend the prawns. Fun to share a lot if dishes, beautiful location
reviews.author: Amber

reviews.text: Really beautiful aesthetics! Kind staff and manager. Ordered an eggs Benedict couldn’t finish it because the hash brown was really hard. Feel like they can improve the food part of things. The eggs Benedict could have had more stuff on it and more flavour felt like stuff were a bit overpriced but it is Cape Town after all.
reviews.author: Nokuzola Nokoyo

reviews.text: Visited for the first time. Ordered an iced coffee and chocolate cake. The chocolate cake was quite underwhelming and dry, did not enjoy it.
reviews.author: Clio Chiriga

reviews.text: Booked for their restaurant week special! Totally worth it. The staff was amazingly helpful and friendly. The atmosphere was great. The food was to die for! I enjoyed from the starter to dessert. It was so fresh and quite generous. The cocktails (especially the paloma) was every girls dream!
reviews.author: Kamva Socikwa

reviews.text:
The cauli bites are a must-try, definitely the highlight of our dinner! I was slightly disappointed by the Paloma I ordered, it didn't taste like grapefruit enough for my liking and the tajin rim didn't taste like tajin. For mains I had the baby chicken and chips, my partner had the lamb chops and roasted sweet potato. The sweet potato was so good and so different from the ones we have in England!
We had the Malva pudding, which was lovely, with an espresso martini... best espresso martini I've ever had!
Our waiter Gibson was kind and gave good recommendations. Thank you!
reviews.author: Coralie Becaud

reviews.text: Came for Restaurant week and we enjoyed it. I chose the Durban curry mussels which I enjoyed. However the line fish was overcooked  but I enjoyed everything else that came with it. The highlight was definitely the hazelnut fondant. It was superb with the hazelnut oozing out. Good service as well and beautiful view.
reviews.author: Tiffany P.

reviews.text: The food is lovely, I enjoyed their steak. Reasonably priced and delicious wines, lovely service from our waiter, Simba. The view is nice and we could take great pictures from the balcony, the lighting is fantastic.
reviews.author: Precious Nondanyana

reviews.text:
Get. The. Riblets!😁
The staff was amazing, incredibly friendly, too, & the service was at a good pace. I’m biased against Sea Point restaurants as I never actively include them when wanting a meal or as an option to try whichever new restaurant - either the food is underwhelming or the service is horrendous at most of the spots in Sea Point. But I was convinced to try out Shoreditch, & boy am I glad that I did.

I think eating their fries should be a rite of passage to… adulting, delicious!
Some things were a miss but I appreciated the generous portions & bold, tasty flavours in some - unexpected - dishes!
A good place to visit!
reviews.author: Tash N.

reviews.text:
The atmosphere is lovely, especially if you sit in the courtyard, but unfortunately, that’s where the positives ended for us.

We booked a table for 8, and despite receiving two confirmation calls, the table still wasn’t ready when we arrived. Drinks took around 20 minutes to arrive, and while the food came out just warm, it was cold by the time the sauces were finally brought to the table.

For a restaurant with such an incredible view and location, the service really needs to improve. It was slow, disorganized, and not up to standard.
reviews.author: Jannae Jackson

reviews.text: We were not staying in the hotel but wanted to have breakfast at the restaurant on Monday, 15 September 2025. The lady at the door to the restaurant was very rude. We loved the food but she really needs to be more inviting and just not speak to you as if you are bothering or interrupting her.
reviews.author: Chantelle Brits

reviews.text: We had breakfast in the sea-facing patio and greatly appreciated being able to place our order just before noon (when breakfast closes). Excellent coffees and breakfast selection. Just to mention that the shakshuka breakfast has way too much onions in it, so best to avoid if you’re not a fan. Excellent views across the prom and of the paragliders coming in. Def worth a visit.
reviews.author: Chrissie P

reviews.text: My friends and I went here for a birthday dinner. The restaurant has a beautiful view of the beach and we were able to watch the sunset, which was gorgeous. The restaurant itself is beautiful and the food was great. Their cocktails were a bit on the pricier side.
reviews.author: Kylie Ndagijimana

reviews.text:
음식은 그냥 저냥 나쁘지 않은
피쉬타코와 라거생맥주
분위기는 ㅇㅋ
안뜰 분위기도 좋은듯
reviews.author: Daya

reviews.text: I love this place very much. Very beautiful design. Tasty food and great service🌷
reviews.author: Olga Bykovs

reviews.text: Went for my birthday brunch, the service was extremely slow although the restaurant was not busy at all, the waiter took forever to bring food. Finally when the food came, she brought overcooked/burnt fries🙄. My experience was not pleasant at all👎👎👎👎
reviews.author: Abigail Tsolo

reviews.text: I had a cheese board and an espresso martini. The board was very good, the martini was fine, but you could tell it barely had any alcohol in it. The staff were nice, as were views, both from indoor and outdoor seating.
reviews.author: Heather S.

reviews.text:
I had the pleasure of spending a luxurious afternoon at this stunning restaurant, nestled inside a luxurious hotel. The atmosphere was refined and sophisticated, with impeccable service that was both attentive and efficient.

But what truly made my experience unforgettable was the dramatic view of the storm rolling in over the sea and the rain lashing against the windows, this created the perfect accompaniment to a relaxing afternoon.

To enhance the experience, I opted for a rich and creamy chi latte, which paired exquisitely with a warm, freshly baked focaccia. The combination was absolute perfection, and I savored every sip and bite.

If you're looking for a luxurious and relaxing dining experience with exceptional service, look no further.
reviews.author: Ameen Bulbulia

reviews.text:
Visited in June 2025.
South Africa. Cape Town 🇿🇦

This cozy restaurant is located inside a hotel building in Seapoint. The interior is beautifully designed, featuring parquet floors, large interesting chandeliers, and pleasant pastel tones. There is also a lovely veranda, but we chose to sit inside the main dining area. The panoramic windows let in plenty of natural light.

FOOD:

The menu offers a mix of European and Mediterranean cuisine. There are small bites, sharing plates, and various main courses. We ordered oysters in the signature sauce — the oysters themselves were firm and fresh, and the sauce complemented them perfectly with an interesting and unique flavor. We also tried a few appetizers: sando with ribs. When ordering, we expected elegant, small Japanese-inspired mini sandwiches. However, these turned out to be regular sandwiches of fairly large size. The toasted bread was tasty, the rib meat juicy, and surprisingly, the fries were delicious, though quite heavy. Another dish was fish tacos — soft homemade tortillas, fish in a thin, crispy batter with avocado and creamy mousse — a good balance of flavors and juicy fish. We really enjoyed this dish.

SERVICE:
The service was excellent. Our waitress was friendly and helped us choose items from the menu. The dishes arrived fairly quickly. We especially appreciated the glass of champagne we ordered, which was poured almost to the brim!

VERDICT:
A great little restaurant in Seapoint. There aren’t many dining options in this area, so if you find yourself nearby, I definitely recommend stopping by. We had a wonderful time.
reviews.author: Olga Maevskaia

reviews.text: The staff are very attentive to each guests needs - Kuda, Lawrence, A… (lovely Capetonian)
reviews.author: Damodara Baliga

reviews.text: EXCELLENT FOOD. lovely hotel cafe. ocean view patio, (very noisy…) overall great spot!
reviews.author: christopher james

reviews.text: Absolutely amazing experience here our first night in Cape Town. This place should have a 5 star rating on Google. Our waitress was wonderful and gave us top-tier amazing service - asking if we wanted more bread for our hummus, bringing lemon for our sparkling water, spacing out the courses perfectly, wiping down our table before dessert, etc. At every interaction and opportunity, she nailed it. Additionally, the food was exceptional. We had the hummus to start, which was delicious with fresh za’tar bread, the tuna sashimi (out of this world flavors and generous portion!), lamb which my husband says was excellent, truffle fries which were the perfect amount of crispy, and the chocolate and hazelnut fondant dessert. Everything was incredible. Still thinking about the tuna sashimi.
reviews.author: Samantha

reviews.text: Unser Essen war fantastisch! Service super. Dessert auch mmmh
reviews.author: alexa haeusler

reviews.text:
Went in January (not sure if the menu has changed since then) - food was amazing (forgot to take pictures because we couldn't wait to dig in) - Had Ribeye steak and a vegetarian option which was curried cauliflower bites.
For drinks, we had Aperol Spritz and a margarita (both were really good too and not overpowering).

For dessert we had Pavlova (and I honestly can't remember what else) - loved every bite of it!

Service was a tad bit slow but waitstaff were amazing 👏🏻 and the atmosphere was good and the setting is undeniably beautiful - honestly worth it for a quick summer dinner, especially if you get to catch the sunset.

Would recommend.
reviews.author: Kaela Hendricks

reviews.text: The service was great. Zukie and Linda went above and beyond to make it a great experience- both were smiling faces I looked forward to seeing in the morning for breakfast while staying at the Winchester.
reviews.author: Vanessa Sekhon

reviews.text:
The menu is so promising, but they really are not delivering. Ordered oysters that I expected to be fresh, but they were cooked (not mentioned in the menu), but still cold and not great. Ordered flamed tuna sashimi - also unimpressed as there did not seem to be anything flamed and the flavors did not go well together at all. A weird mix of everything that cancels the flavor out. Fish was not too fresh. Cauli bites were equally bad. Half-grilled cauliflower in a confusing mix of flavors. Really below average. Despite being hungry, I could not finish my dish.
It’s a shame because the place is really beautiful and could benefit a lot from a proper restaurant. I really hope they make significant improvements in the kitchen and with their ingredients as it is a beautiful place and has so much potential. The 3 point something google rating is really not misleading at the moment.
reviews.author: Juuli Hiio

reviews.text: I love the space, the waiters are amazing as well as the food🙌🏽
reviews.author: Noemi Weber

reviews.text:
I was told by a member of staff that the business keeps the tips rather than paying it to staff.

Tip in cash!!!!

This is a despicable practice and if I could give zero stars I most certainly would.
reviews.author: Brett Mortensen

reviews.text: Very good food, nice vibe and lovely courtyard. Waiters were knowledgable and friendly.
reviews.author: Henrike Schaumann

reviews.text: The service was desperately slow - and my meal was expensive and VERY disappointing - I had line-fish which was charred and the size of a postage stamp when it eventually arrived… I had been before, and was expecting better - won’t be back.
reviews.author: Jenny Leslie

reviews.text: Service was very slow. Went for breakfast and ended up having lunch ! A bit of a rip off too. Wine offered (Kaapzicht Chenin) had 3 ranges. Charge premium, but served bottom range at 400%
reviews.author: Paul anley

reviews.text:
The restaurant is quite fancy and nice
I like the views next to the sea.
reviews.author: Eliseè Longo

reviews.text: The food was reasonably priced and the taste impeccable. The desserts were out of this world! The view is amazing ✨️
reviews.author: Fai Shabs

reviews.text: Excellent restaurant with a beautiful view of the Atlantic Ocean.
reviews.author: Mattheus Wessels

reviews.text: So tired of not being acknowledged or greeted when arriving at a restaurant. Ignored by staff until I asked if I could sit anywhere. Waitron was lovely once we were seated. Had a delicious lunch with wine. However there was no milk for cappucino - disappointing and amazing that no one could get milk from a store until their stock arrived. Who is in charge of stock levels here? It’s the festive season! Sigh.
reviews.author: Jane Wright

reviews.text: Excellent food and service
reviews.author: Alec Davis

reviews.text: Beautiful place, amazing food, fair prices, okay service
reviews.author: Bongi Motaung

reviews.text:
Great ambience
Amazing service
Delicious food and cocktails
Can highly recommend.
reviews.author: Kianeeka Bastians

reviews.text: I had a frustrating experience with restaurant. The representative didn't listen to my concerns, resulting in an incorrect booking. On top of that, the telephonic etiquette was poor—no clear communication, and the call was unprofessional. Overall, a very disappointing experience.
reviews.author: Chanelle Smith

reviews.text: We took our daughter for breakfast yesterday.  Such a wonderful experience.  She was so thrilled to receive a beautiful plate afterwards to wish her a happy birthday!
reviews.author: Maria Mackenzie

reviews.text: Enjoyed my experience....
reviews.author: nolan charles

reviews.text:
Food was ok, but the service was not good. The lady with red hair messed up our order taking too long the food for our soon.
I must say the place is beautiful with the flowers around
reviews.author: Jose Urbina

reviews.text: Perfect! 👌
reviews.author: Duane Daré

reviews.text: Good food and service.
reviews.author: Philip Thornton

reviews.text: The food didn’t wow me , it’s average , I was disappointed in my lamb chops , definitely had better . Tried some of the tapas my friend had , was also nice but nothing WOW . My partner’s peri peri baby chicken was tasty. Had the bread pudding 🍮 which i thoroughly enjoyed that , they should be more generous with the custard . Great vibes though , views of the ocean 🌊.
reviews.author: Tapiwa Risai Kahari

reviews.text: As a local, proud to say this is a Sea Point icon rightfully. Enjoyed delicious sharing plates on the terrace watching paragliders land right in front of us. Service was attentive and friendly. Definitely go for the burrata and wagyu sliders. The cocktails are worth a go also!
reviews.author: Lisa Hunneyball

reviews.text: Lovely spot for breakfast but the buffet could include some eggs, bacon and toast and the coffee’s could be bigger. Otherwise divine location in the hotel and superb service 👌🏼
reviews.author: Kayleigh Spanidis

reviews.text: Horrible place, poorest service in town, takes 20 minutes to get a glass of wine in front of you and 40 minutes for a sorry portion of chicken wings. Never coming back.
reviews.author: Gideon Klipstein

reviews.text: We sat in the atrium and it is a beautiful backdrop for a great meal. We had brunch and our service was good. Attentive but not hovering. The highlights were the mussels, risotto, lamb riblets, and focaccia. Definitely get a glass or two of Chenin Blanc or Pinotage. A must try if visiting Sea Point for the first time
reviews.author: Frantz Joseph

reviews.text:
Delightful. Enjoyed everything—mussels, burrata, risotto, Caesar salad, focaccia (a highlight), and cabbage (another highlight).

Service was excellent.
reviews.author: Jamaica Aali

reviews.text: The food was phenomenal but the service was slightly slow.  Can’t complain about the Seapoint sea views at all though.
reviews.author: Brynn Gerson

reviews.text: Fantastic experience, great service and the food was simple and outstanding — harisa scramble, a mimosa & an almond milk flat white.
reviews.author: paige rigby

reviews.text: Quite disappointed by this experience. I ordered the seared salmon. Let me tell you the salmon was not seared at all and just raw. How does one argue this? And on top of that the salmon smelled and tasted extremely fishy. I eat sushi and raw salmon all the time and never once experienced this  again something you can't complain about because the waiter says we'll it's raw that's how it's supposed to be. The food was a big turn off. My friend had the mushroom risotto and that wasn't anything to brag about either. Exorbitant prices for below average. By far the worst cocktail I had and I spent like R160 for a cocktail that didn't even taste good. Poor service, poor quality of food only good thing is the atmosphere and ambience. I've nothing good to say about this restaurant.
reviews.author: leshna naidoo

reviews.text: Awesome meal with my family. Really friendly and helpful service. Cocktails were incredible as was the food, great job.
reviews.author: Ryan Stobie

reviews.text:
We booked way in advance and paid the deposit (16 ppl)and went there physically to confirm menu prices we saw online and everything was confirmed .

To our surprise when our bill comes it's way over our budget and the reason is that the pricing we saw online is completely different to the pricing on their actual menu and that's not their problem because they do not put those menu online

On top of that they never deducted our deposit from the actual total cost so as we are sitting here they owe us our deposit back.

Very shitty Manager or whatever Rauri is.
reviews.author: Yonela Esethu

reviews.text: Avrage food and service at a high price.
reviews.author: L

reviews.text: I love this place.....
reviews.author: Stef Schlecht

reviews.text: I had the Salmon Bruschetta on the breaki menu! It’s my favourite breakfast right now in CT. Definitely worth a visit. Their fresh juices are also great and customisable
reviews.author: Jacques De Kock

reviews.text: This is a great restaurant within the hotel, with views of the ocean next to the street. Great food selection, really fun staff, and definitely needs a visit if you're in the area.
reviews.author: Ryan Altorfer

reviews.text: I'm tempted to keep this place under wraps just to ensure I can always snag a reservation—haha! Our very first time having a meal at Shoreditch was nothing short of spectacular. The waiter truly went the extra mile to ensure our comfort, serving us with  patience and attention. Despite the gamble of booking a table for 10 on Mother's Day at an uncharted venue, the experience exceeded all expectations. The cuisine and atmosphere were impeccable, and surprisingly affordable given the upscale locale. The courtyard exudes charm, boasting a romantic ambiance that left us enchanted. Without a doubt, this has become my go-to spot for breakfast!
reviews.author: Lorraine Rhoda

reviews.text: Staff is very kind, and food was great. Had breakfast one day and lunch in another day. Both were delicious.
reviews.author: Tiago Cunha

reviews.text: It's a beautiful place, which is why I believe it attracts a lot of people, but the service is practically non-existent. We had to ask for service multiple times and saw a few other tables where people actually had to physically get up and go and request service from the waitrons. This is also the second time I've been there and unfortunately had the same experience last time too.
reviews.author: Catherine YM

reviews.text: Beautiful scenic coffee shop in the courtyard of the hotel. We ordered bacon eggs Benedict and a side of pancakes.  Breakfast served till 12pm. Inside seating available on cold and rainy days.
reviews.author: Roelien Venter

reviews.text:
A beautiful restaurant inside the Winchester Hotel, we visited a good few times already, its a must when in CT. We always sit at the atrium, in the center of the hotel, it has a lovely colonial appearance, like when in Rome. The manager is very friendly and will make sure you are taken care of.
A very modern style menu, you can also order in small plates so you can have a taste of many dishes. Very delicious food. The hummus and flat bread is amazing, maybe the best out there. Few different style flavored wings, tasty burgers, deserts and great coffee, in Sea Point, right across the ocean, simply the best situation to be in.
Photos from different visits.
Please go and see / eat for yourselves. Highly recommended.
reviews.author: Radesh N

reviews.text: We had an amazing waiter… by the name of John’s took care of a group of 5 difficult girls… but handled it like a damn pro. And the hostess (didn’t get the name but she was so patient) and the general staff was super friendly)
reviews.author: Nasiphi Matoni

reviews.text: Food was great, nice outdoor seating. A lovely evening.
reviews.author: Jacqui Benson-Mabombo

reviews.text:
I’ve been staying at the Winchester the last few days and as part of this I’ve been eating at Shoreditch every morning and also spent my final evening having dinner too.

This place is amazing and I would recommend it to anyone who is lucky to be in the area.

I would also like to say a special thanks to John who made our final evening very personal with his friendly and warm approach.
reviews.author: Gavin

reviews.text: The food and service was amazing! The lamb chops were so tasty and the chicken skewers, oysters and fish tacos were great too. All the staff were so kind and attentive throughout the whole meal. Would definitely recommend!
reviews.author: Brittany Smith

reviews.text: After waiting almost a halfhour for the waitress to take our drink order, we gave up and went on to another restaurant.  We were on the front porch, which was lovely, bur the lack of service was appalling.  The waitress had handed us two menus, but then simply disappeared.  Too bad.  In prior years we loved having drinks and sometimes dinner at the Winchester.
reviews.author: Peter Brink

reviews.text: My friends and I had a lovely time at Shoreditch last Sunday. The place is extremely beautiful and the staff welcomed us quite warmly, even though we were 20 minutes late for our reservation. It's a lovely place to have lunch with family and friends. My friends had the beef short ribs along with the hand cut fries and chilli mayo, whilst I had the beef short rib sandwich with hand cut fries as well. Both meals were extremely delicious, and I love that we didn't wait too long for our food. The only thing I can complain about is that there wasn't a menu for the cocktails we ordered. We had to ask our waiter about them based on what another customer was drinking. But other than that, it's a beautiful place with amazing food. I would definitely recommend and come back again!
reviews.author: Mandile Mbambiso

reviews.text:
A walk along the promenade found us at the Winchester Mantions, home of the restaurant. It has great views, a Para gliding landing zone across the road and a relaxed atmosphere.

The menu had changed since our last visit, and a recent upgrade, so we took some time to choose.
I went for the Greek Salad and a charcoal fired sweet potato. My partner took the flat bread and a red pepper Humus with some Chilli. I forget it's official name as at first we thought it came with a variety of accompanyments but actually only one is available in the listed price. The menu listed three brands of Sparkling water but it turned out only the most expensive was available. We voted for water.
The service was excellent and in short the food (and water) also most enjoyable. The Greek Salad had a fair share of unexpected lettuce but on such a sunny and still day we were very happy with our choices.
reviews.author: Richard Majewski

reviews.text: The food is top class and very tasty. It is in a great location close to the famous Sea Point promenade. I came to watch soccer with friends at the bar and we ordered some food. The hummus and flat bread was amazing as well as the chicken skewers.
reviews.author: Mark Stillerman

reviews.text: The breakfast dishes are unique and tasty- though the portion sizes do not match the prices. Next time we will definitely opt for the breakfast buffet. The setting is obviously beautiful, especially on the front porch. The staff are fantastic.
reviews.author: Carene Mouton

reviews.text: Sat in the atrium and the flowers were great. My wife and I split the bone in rib-eye,  prawn soup, pit roasted cauliflower, pit roasted sweet potato,  and pit roasted cabbage.  For us meat was perfect and the cauliflower stole the show. The cabbage was tasty but needs to be a bit softer. Overall a great experience so would definitely return.
reviews.author: Joseph Russow

reviews.text: Beautiful setting, absolutely love the ambience. Great service and friendly staff. Amazing starters but the Mains were a real let down. But Dessert made up for that. Great place to get drinks and nibble on some tapas with friends
reviews.author: Ntandokazi Sishange

reviews.text: Second time here for tapas and drinks, it is a great spot for a sunny afternoon, their courtyard is absolutely stunning and the cheese board is great. We had a lovely waiter called John and his service was immaculate.
reviews.author: Mugisha Hakizimana

reviews.text:
A classy and comfortable environment.
Lovely for a nice lunch on a Sunday.

They have a pretty good selection of puddings 😍
reviews.author: Liam Vee

reviews.text: Popped in here for a quick,  delicious  iced caramel coffee. I sat in the courtyard which was nice and cool. My server's name was Gift and he was very helpful
reviews.author: simone frasca

reviews.text: Amazing. They have different areas to sit in. Indoors, 2 different places to sit if you want to be outside. If you want people to see you and watch the sunset you can sit outside, in from of the restaurant. If you want to sit outside but in a secluded area. They’ve got you, there’s an inside area with not roof top which is simply amazing.
reviews.author: Elmo Monese

reviews.text: Service was excellent..food was good..baked  cheesecake was the best..and nobody can beat the view..best especially in a lovely day
reviews.author: Geraldene Michaels

reviews.text: Beautiful hotel and restaurant, the food menu is a bit limited but it’s ok I guess. Friendly staff members. Call and make a reservation before you go or you won’t find a table
reviews.author: L B

reviews.text: Just passed by for a sundowner and got stuck - atmosphere, very good service, nice drinks and tasty food.  Our waitress  was a treasure with all her recommendations - thank you again!
reviews.author: H. Scharf

reviews.text: Lovely atmosphere in the inner courtyard with monochrome decor. Interesting snack menu, attentive staff
reviews.author: Lindie White

reviews.text: A nice restaurant inside the Winchester Hotel. The service was good and provided by friendly staff. The drinks options are good and the food sampled was delicious. Although you may order a fish taco and be surprised by the cracker-like discs with a small structure of fine food atop, they taste great. The view of the ocean is grand and the overall atmosphere provides a good place to spend a few hours with friends.
reviews.author: Joe D Ape

reviews.text: We had such an amazing experience. I wish I could thank the chef personally. It's not easy to be impressive with breakfast but our meals were outstanding. The staff is so friendly and accommodating. A special thanks you to Patric our waiter for making our Cape town experience feel even better
reviews.author: Palesa Mokoena

reviews.text:
From the first point of entry to the building the door staff were unaware.
We waited 10min before calling someone to take our order as no one had come to the table, our coffee then took 20min to receive and our food 40min… hate to be so negative but the food was bland and the eggs Benedict key ingredient hollandaise sauce was flavourless and bland. No ambiance in the restaurant (not even gentle music). Overall just a poor experience and should the owners like more constructive feedback then I’ll be happy to afford the time. The location has potential.
reviews.author: smack450

reviews.text: Very dissatisfied . Ordered a a Cape Malay pickled fish starter and received 2 crumbed fish sosaties . There was a small charcoal burning in the a small iron pot which gave the sosatie a disgusting bitter taste . That was not what I ordered . Complained to the waiter and he said that he had spoken to his manager. We were charged for the disgusting meal and the manager never communicated with us . I am very unhappy.
reviews.author: Carlos Quintal

reviews.text: Even though we were served in a friendly manner and the ambience is really nice, we were unfortunately served bad meat. This cost us two days of our holiday due to a stomach illness, which I do not want to go into detail about.
reviews.author: Mimi

reviews.text: We had great lunch and all but found out the next day I left something behind, called  the same number we had used to book to see if anyone picked up anything. Well we were not helped at all.Customer service from whoever who answers the phone, could be management I don’t know but it’s extremely ridiculous. Wasn’t very helpful at all!!!
reviews.author: adrine chengetai

reviews.text: The food was really disappointing and the menu is mis leading, the waiters and the space were really nice
reviews.author: Anne-Marie Bothma

reviews.text:
Only go here for drinks .
Overrated overpriced limited menu ridiculously small portions.
They could learn a thing or two from nv80!
reviews.author: Mishkah Gaffoor
